headline: System integrator Guoxia’s revenue and profits surge over 100% amidst shift to utility-scale
summary_80w: Energy storage firm Guoxia Technology (02655.HK) has released its first full annual report since listing on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange late last year.

headline: NSW planners approve Foresight’s 150MWh BESS; Banpu submits 1GWh development to Australia’s EPBC Act
summary_80w: A 150MWh system has been granted approval in Australia, whilst a 1,000MWh system has been submitted for federal consideration.

headline: Powering Australia’s next phase of decarbonisation: Why long-duration storage must lead
summary_80w: James Costello, CEO of EORA Energy, argues that long-duration vanadium redox flow battery storage is critical to Western Australia's decarbonisation efforts, particularly for remote mining operations.

headline: ‘Europe’s solar market is being shaped by co-location, regulation and growing tension’
summary_80w: Q&A: Sarah Montgomery, founder & CEO of Infyos, gives her take on the rise of co-location and growing tension in Europe's solar market.

headline: California regulator rejects community solar pricing model, triggering industry backlash
summary_80w: The California Public Utilities Commission (CPUC) has issued a proposed decision rejecting a solar industry-backed Net Value Billing Tariff (NVBT) for community solar programmes, and instead advancing a compensation framework based on the Avoided Cost Calculator (ACC).

headline: European solar PV module price increase in March 2026 ‘may prove temporary’, says sun.store
summary_80w: The selling price of solar PV module technology types in Europe has continued to increase in March 2026, according to the latest report from online solar marketplace sun.store.

headline: Levanta Renewables awards EPC contract to China Energy Engineering Group
summary_80w: Singapore-based renewables firm Levanta Renewables has signed an engineering, procurement and construction (EPC) contract with China Energy Engineering Group (CEEC) for a solar-plus-storage project in the Philippines.

headline: Maine passes balcony solar law, Virginia and Colorado to follow
summary_80w: Signed into law by governor Janet Mills on 6 April 2026, LD 1730 allows the installation of plug-in systems of up to 1,200 watts.

headline: India becomes third largest country for solar PV capacity
summary_80w: India has become the third-largest country by installed renewable energy capacity, reaching 274.68, with over 150GW of solar PV capacity, according to statistics from the Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE).

headline: Array to supply OmniTrack solar trackers for Pekintas’ 260MW solar project
summary_80w: Array Technologies will deploy its OmniTrack terrain-following tracker system at a 260MW solar PV project being developed by Turkish company Pekintas.

headline: Why fundamental research in photovoltaics remains critical for an established technology
summary_80w: Despite PV’s maturity, a new paper argues that its growing global significance makes ongoing research essential.

headline: Australia’s Bellevue Gold achieves 90% renewable energy at off-grid mine in March
summary_80w: Bellevue Gold has reported that approximately 90% of its electricity demand at its Western Australian gold mine was met by renewable energy in March 2026.

headline: Minnesota regulator approves Xcel’s controversial utility-owned virtual power plant
summary_80w: The Minnesota Public Utilities Commission (MPUC) has approved a 200MW expansion of utility Xcel Energy’s Capacity*Connect virtual power plant (VPP) programme.

headline: American Battery Factory, Lion Energy form partnership for US-made LFP BESS equipment

headline: TenneT signs contract with developer for ‘first controllable congestion mitigator’ BESS project in Netherlands
summary_80w: Dutch TSO TenneT has signed a contract with a developer for a large-scale BESS which will help to relieve grid congestion, possibly enabling the quicker deployment of renewables in the Netherlands.

headline: Australia: Battery storage cushions AU$1 billion fuel shock as Climate Council demands May budget action
summary_80w: Australia's heavy reliance on imported fossil fuels has left households and businesses dangerously exposed to global energy shocks, with the current conflict involving the US and Iran costing motorists more than AU$1 billion (US$710 million) in March alone.

headline: ‘75% of what we need is not there’: Australian states race to deploy gigawatts of battery storage

headline: Energy Vault the latest international player to tap Japan’s BESS market potential

headline: Italy ‘definitely’ most attractive European market for solar development

headline: Turkey must triple solar and wind capacity to meet 2035 targets – Ember

headline: Reno planning commission approves permit for 200MW Trego Grid BESS project

summary_80w: MetaWealth COO Michael Topolinski IV discussed the firm’s first BESS project in Romania, which is partially financed with bonds marketed at retail investors.
